title: dodonaea concinna
intro: Dodonaea concinna, also known as the small-leaved hop-bush, is a shrub native to Australia and is a member of the Sapindaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dodonaea concinna include its ability to treat fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Dodonaea concinna include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water or a solvent, or by mixing them with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
